Constitutional ProvisionsVacancies in the House of Representatives, see Const. Art. I, § 2, cl. 4.

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (b), the time for holding elections in any State, District, or Territory for a Representative or Delegate to fill a vacancy, whether such vacancy is caused by a failure to elect at the time prescribed by law, or by the death, resignation, or incapacity of a person elected, may be prescribed by the laws of the several States and Territories respectively.

children.heading: Special rules in extraordinary circumstances
children.label: b Special rules in extraordinary circumstances

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In extraordinary circumstances, the executive authority of any State in which a vacancy exists in its representation in the House of Representatives shall issue a writ of election to fill such vacancy by special election.

children.label: A
children.text: a regularly scheduled general election for the office involved is to be held; or

children.label: B
children.text: another special election for the office involved is to be held, pursuant to a writ for a special election issued by the chief executive of the State prior to the date of the announcement of the vacancy.

children.label: A
children.text: by nominations made not later than 10 days after the Speaker announces that the vacancy exists by the political parties of the State that are authorized by State law to nominate candidates for the election; or

children.label: B
children.text: by any other method the State considers appropriate, including holding primary elections, that will ensure that the State will hold the special election within the deadline required under paragraph (2).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In this subsection, “extraordinary circumstances” occur when the Speaker of the House of Representatives announces that vacancies in the representation from the States in the House exceed 100.

children.label: i
children.text: Not later than 2 days after the announcement, the action shall be filed in the United States District Court having jurisdiction in the district of the Member of the House of Representatives whose seat has been announced to be vacant and shall be heard by a 3-judge court convened pursuant to .section 2284 of title 28

children.label: ii
children.text: A copy of the complaint shall be delivered promptly to the Clerk of the House of Representatives.

children.label: iii
children.text: A final decision in the action shall be made within 3 days of the filing of such action and shall not be reviewable.

children.label: iv
children.text: The executive authority of the State that contains the district of the Member of the House of Representatives whose seat has been announced to be vacant shall have the right to intervene either in support of or opposition to the position of a party to the case regarding the announcement of such vacancy.

children.heading: Protecting ability of absent military and overseas voters to participate in special elections
children.label: 5 Protecting ability of absent military and overseas voters to participate in special elections

children.heading: Deadline for transmittal of absentee ballots
children.label: A Deadline for transmittal of absentee ballots
children.text: In conducting a special election held under this subsection to fill a vacancy in its representation, the State shall ensure to the greatest extent practicable (including through the use of electronic means) that absentee ballots for the election are transmitted to absent uniformed services voters and overseas voters (as such terms are defined in the Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act [ et seq.]) not later than 15 days after the Speaker of the House of Representatives announces that the vacancy exists.52 U.S.C. 20301

children.heading: Period for ballot transit time
children.label: B Period for ballot transit time
children.text: Notwithstanding the deadlines referred to in paragraphs (2) and (3), in the case of an individual who is an absent uniformed services voter or an overseas voter (as such terms are defined in the Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act), a State shall accept and process any otherwise valid ballot or other election material from the voter so long as the ballot or other material is received by the appropriate State election official not later than 45 days after the State transmits the ballot or other material to the voter.

children.heading: Application to District of Columbia and territories
children.label: 6 Application to District of Columbia and territories
A
children.text: to a Delegate or Resident Commissioner to the Congress in the same manner as it applies to a Member of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: B
children.text: to the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, American Samoa, Guam, and the United States Virgin Islands in the same manner as it applies to a State, except that a vacancy in the representation from any such jurisdiction in the House shall not be taken into account by the Speaker in determining whether vacancies in the representation from the States in the House exceed 100 for purposes of paragraph (4)(A).

children.heading: Rule of construction regarding Federal election laws
children.label: 7 Rule of construction regarding Federal election laws
A
children.text: The Voting Rights Act of 1965 ( et seq.) [now et seq.], as amended.42 U.S.C. 197352 U.S.C. 10301

children.label: B
children.text: The Voting Accessibility for the Elderly and Handicapped Act ( et seq.) [now et seq.], as amended.42 U.S.C. 1973ee52 U.S.C. 20101

children.label: C
children.text: The Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act ( et seq.) [now et seq.], as amended.42 U.S.C. 1973ff52 U.S.C. 20301

children.label: D
children.text: The National Voter Registration Act of 1993 ( et seq.) [now et seq.], as amended.42 U.S.C. 1973gg52 U.S.C. 20501

children.label: E
children.text: The Americans With Disabilities Act of 1990 ( et seq.), as amended.42 U.S.C. 12101

children.label: F
children.text: The Rehabilitation Act of 1973 ( et seq.), as amended.29 U.S.C. 701

children.label: G
children.text: The Help America Vote Act of 2002 ( et seq.) [now et seq.], as amended.42 U.S.C. 1530152 U.S.C. 20901
